name: Dezeen
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Dezeen's design language — the world's most-read architecture-and-design magazine built on a strict monochrome white-and-near-black canvas, a custom serif (Dezeen Display) for every headline, and a 'photograph IS the page' approach where 4-column masonry grids of beautifully-shot architecture imagery carry the entire visual weight. The brand reads as 'design publication of record' — confident, image-forward, anti-chrome.

design_language.motion_signature: Almost no motion — 150ms ease-out on hover; signature is the 4-column masonry grid loading images on scroll at 200ms fade-in stagger; image hover at 110% scale over 300ms within fixed crop frame.

trademark_signals: Photograph-as-page — 4-column masonry grid of architecture/design photography carrying ~80% of the visual surface area, every page
Dezeen Display custom serif at every headline tier — weight 500 with -0.01em tracking, sizes 24–48px — refuses the all-sans architecture-publication default
Strict black-on-white duet — no chromatic accent, no gradient, no decorative chrome; the photography supplies all color
Image hover micro-zoom — images scale to 110% within a fixed crop frame on hover, the brand's one motion signature
Square or rectangular image tiles — refuses the rounded-corner architecture-publication trend
ai_slop_avoided: Don't introduce a chromatic brand accent. The brand is monochrome chrome — the photography supplies all color
Don't substitute the serif headlines with a sans face. Dezeen Display serif IS the brand voice
Don't round image corners. The brand keeps strict rectangular geometry
Don't reduce the masonry grid density. 4-column at desktop is the brand's structural signature
Don't add drop shadows on image tiles. Surface contrast and hairlines carry elevation
